pr.title: 088: Manifest Schema Pipeline — complete spec implementation

comments.body: You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews. You can see your limits in the [Codex usage dashboard](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage).

Review Summary by Qodo

Manifest Schema Pipeline & Ingest Runtime — Complete Spec Implementation with Full Test Coverage ✨ Enhancement 🧪 Tests 📝 Documentation

Walkthroughs

Description 
  **Core Implementation**
• Completes 088-manifest-schema-pipeline spec with Pydantic v0 models, JSON Schema export, and full
  validator with secret detection and PII redaction enforcement
• Implements ReaderAdapter protocol and registry system with built-in adapters for GitHub, Google
  Drive, local directories, and Confluence
• Delivers ManifestPipeline orchestrator for validate→plan→fetch→transform→embed→upsert workflow
  with error handling policies
• Adds evaluation metrics (hitRate@k, ndcg@k) with threshold-based pass/fail determination for
  retrieval quality gates
  **CLI & Orchestration**
• New manifest CLI command group with validate, plan, run, and evaluate subcommands
• Fixes manifest ingest failure policy handling and concurrency validation (1-500 bounds) in
  Temporal workflow
• Adds manifest CLI helpers for validation, planning, execution, and evaluation operations
  **Testing & Validation**
• 109 manifest validator and pipeline tests covering schema validation, cross-field references,
  secret detection, PII enforcement, and adapter contracts
• 81 manifest ingest integration tests for workflow orchestration, child-run lineage, idempotency,
  and large fan-out persistence
• 28 dashboard tests for manifest status endpoints and pagination
• Comprehensive RAG service tests for budget enforcement, settings validation, guardrails,
  telemetry, and CLI argument parsing
  **Documentation & Specifications**
• Complete manifest system operator guide with schema definitions, examples, and CLI reference
• Manifest ingest runtime spec (070) with 33 implementation tasks, data model, OpenAPI contract, and
  architectural decisions
• Manifest schema pipeline spec (088) with 29 implementation tasks, requirements traceability, and
  design research
• RAG worker task completion tracking (036) marking 18 of 29 tasks as complete
• JSON Schema export for manifest v0 with all validation constraints
  **Cross-Spec Coherence**
• Verified clean boundaries between 036 (retrieval), 070 (control plane), and 088 (data plane)
• Mapped all 16 DOC-REQ requirements (070) and 9 DOC-REQ requirements (088) to implementation and
  validation tasks
• Consolidated RAG documentation with 1:1 mapping between docs and specs

The `ConfluenceReader` is initialized without passing the API token that was resolved from the manifest's `auth` block. The `token` variable is resolved on line 290 but is never used, which will likely cause authentication failures when trying to fetch data from Confluence.

The `ConfluenceReader` from `llama-index-readers-confluence` should be initialized with the token.

```suggestion
            reader = ConfluenceReader(
                base_url=base_url,
                api_token=token,
                cloud=True,
            )
```

The abridged JSON schema example for `dataSources` items marks `params` as a required field. However, the Pydantic model `DataSourceConfig` defines `params` with a `default_factory`, making it optional, and the generated full JSON schema in `docs/schemas/manifest-v0.json` also reflects this. To maintain consistency and accuracy in the documentation, `params` should be removed from the `required` list in this example.

```suggestion
"required": ["id", "type"],
```

helpers expect relevant_ids while the documented/example dataset format uses gold, blocking
